title: hieracium bifidum subsp. caesiiceps
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. caesiiceps is a subspecies of the Hieracium bifidum plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is also known as the 'mouse-ear hawkweed' and is native to parts of North America and Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of Hieracium bifidum subsp. caesiiceps can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has been used to support immune function and reduce inflammation in the body.
